job_duties_and_previous_employment.describe_your_job_duties:
I currently work as a line service technician and ramp operations lead at a regional FBO in Anchorage. My primary responsibilities include safely marshaling aircraft to and from parking areas, operating tugs and ground support equipment, performing fueling operations on single- and multi-engine airplanes as well as small jets, and assisting with loading and unloading baggage and cargo. On a daily basis I conduct pre- and post-fueling inspections, verify fuel types and quantities with pilots, and complete fuel tickets and log entries accurately.

I am also responsible for maintaining a clean and organized ramp and hangar environment, including snow removal in winter, chocking and tying down aircraft, and ensuring that safety cones, fire extinguishers, and equipment are properly placed and in good working order. Part of my job is to communicate directly with pilots, dispatchers, and maintenance personnel regarding aircraft status, fuel requests, and any ramp issues that might affect scheduling or safety.

In addition, I train and mentor new line service staff on ramp safety procedures, company SOPs, radio communication etiquette, and customer service standards. I frequently use handheld radios to coordinate aircraft movements with the front desk and maintenance, and I follow TSA and airport security protocols, including badge control and escorting requirements. I also assist with basic aircraft servicing such as checking and servicing engine oil, replenishing oxygen, and servicing lavatory systems under the direction of maintenance.

Because we handle both based and transient traffic, I often help customers with ground transportation arrangements (taxis, rental cars, hotel shuttles), answer questions about local conditions and weather, and coordinate with charter operators who are flying tourists and locals to remote locations. During peak summer months, the job can be very fast-paced, and I’m responsible for prioritizing multiple arriving and departing aircraft while maintaining safety margins and avoiding ramp congestion.

Overall, my role combines hands-on physical work on the ramp, safety-critical decision-making, and a strong customer service component, all within a small team environment where clear communication and reliability are essential.
job_duties_and_previous_employment.reason_for_leaving:
I am seeking to transition into a role that is more directly connected to Alaska bush and scenic flying operations, which has been my long-term career interest. While I have learned a tremendous amount in my current FBO position—especially about ramp safety, fueling procedures, and working efficiently in variable weather—I feel that I have progressed as far as I reasonably can in my current organization without relocating out of Alaska or moving into a primarily office-based role.

What specifically motivates me to leave is the desire to work for a company whose primary focus is on flightseeing, remote lodge support, and backcountry operations, rather than general business and corporate aviation. I want to be part of an operation where my day-to-day efforts directly support pilots and passengers heading into the Alaska backcountry, and where I can continue to build experience relevant to eventually pursuing my commercial pilot certificate.

There have not been any disciplinary issues or conflicts leading to this decision. I have a good relationship with my current supervisor and coworkers, and I plan to provide at least two weeks’ notice and assist in training a replacement if needed. I’ve communicated to my supervisor that I am interested in growth opportunities, but given the size and structure of the company, there are limited paths forward that align with my goals.

In short, I am leaving on good terms to pursue a position that better aligns with my aviation career path, offers more direct exposure to the type of flying Rust’s Flying Service and K2 Aviation are known for, and allows me to continue developing both my operational skills and my understanding of Alaska’s unique flying environment.

professional_references_table.name: Sarah L. McKenzie
professional_references_table.occupation: Line Service Supervisor, Northern Horizon FBO
